---
title: "Huawei AI chips used for DeepSeek V4 training, after success in inference - Huawei Central"
date: 2026-06-06 03:56:47 +0000
category: news
subcategory: other
company: "DeepSeek"

classification_confidence: 0.70

layout: post
description: "Huawei's AI chips are now being utilized for training the DeepSeek V4 model, following previous success in inference tasks."
---

Huawei has announced that its AI chips are being deployed for the training of the DeepSeek V4 model, marking a significant step after achieving notable success in inference tasks. This development is particularly relevant as AI companies are increasingly focused on optimizing both training and inference capabilities to enhance model performance and efficiency.

The DeepSeek V4 model has demonstrated impressive results in inference, which involves applying a trained model to new data to generate predictions. This success has paved the way for Huawei to leverage its proprietary AI chips for training, a process that involves teaching the model using large datasets. The transition from inference to training indicates Huawei's confidence in its hardware capabilities and the potential for further advancements in AI applications. As reported by [Huawei Central](https://news.google.?oc=5&hl=en-US&gl=US&ceid=US%3Aen), this shift is expected to enhance the model's capabilities and performance metrics significantly.

Huawei's AI chips are designed to optimize both training and inference processes, which is crucial in a competitive landscape where companies like NVIDIA and Google are also advancing their AI hardware. The ability to efficiently train models is becoming increasingly important as the demand for AI applications grows across various sectors, from healthcare to finance. By investing in its AI chip technology, Huawei aims to position itself as a leader in the AI hardware market, potentially challenging established players.

For users and developers, the successful training of DeepSeek V4 on Huawei's chips could lead to more powerful AI applications that can handle complex tasks with greater efficiency. This development may also attract interest from businesses looking to leverage AI for their operations, as improved model performance can translate into better decision-making and enhanced operational efficiency.

Looking ahead, it will be essential to monitor how DeepSeek V4 performs in real-world applications and whether Huawei's advancements in AI chips can keep pace with the rapidly evolving demands of the AI industry.
